name: use-focus-restore
title: useFocusRestore
description: Capture and restore focus around overlays, panels, command palettes, and triggerless temporary UI with nested stack safety.

content.text: Stable-ref escape hatch: optionsRef is read ONLY inside capture/restore
event-driven callbacks and unmount cleanup (never during render), so
mid-render writes are safe under concurrent rendering. See AGENTS.md

docs.description: Capture and restore focus around overlays, panels, command palettes, and triggerless temporary UI with nested stack safety.

parameters.name: options.enabled
parameters.description: Whether capture and restore are active.

parameters.name: options.restoreOnUnmount
parameters.description: Restore focus during cleanup if capture was called and restore was not called manually.

parameters.name: options.preventScroll
parameters.description: Pass preventScroll to the focus call when restoring focus.

parameters.name: options.fallback
parameters.description: Fallback element to focus when the captured target is unavailable.

returns.type: { capture: (ownerDocument?: Document) => HTMLElement | null; restore: () => boolean; target: HTMLElement | null }
returns.description: capture stores the current focus target, restore focuses the captured or fallback target, and target exposes the last captured element.

notes.title: Hook boundary
notes.content: useFocusRestore is the React hook. getRestorableFocusTarget and restoreFocus are plain DOM utilities for non-hook code.

notes.title: Nested overlays
notes.content: Focus restore entries are stack-aware, so nested overlays restore focus in close order and older layers do not steal focus from newer ones.

content.text: Focus returns to the opener when this panel closes.
Summon opens the palette and captures the current focus. On close, focus
returns there; `fallback` (the editor surface) catches the case where
nothing was focused at open time so focus never lands on <body>.
Closing restores focus to wherever it was, or to the editor surface fallback.
